user_query: Please initialize a structured personal wiki under output/wiki/ and ingest two source documents from input/. Specifically: 1) Read input/articles/acme-2025-update.md and input/papers/supply-chain-brief.md; 2) Create source summary pages at output/wiki/sources/acme-2025-update.md and output/wiki/sources/supply-chain-brief.md with YAML frontmatter including title, type=source, source_type, created, updated, raw_path (pointing to the input/ path), publication_date, authors, and tags; 3) Create or update an entity page output/wiki/entities/acme-corp.md (entity_type=organization) and concept pages output/wiki/concepts/usage-based-pricing.md and output/wiki/concepts/supply-chain-resilience.md with appropriate YAML frontmatter (title, type, concept/entity type, created, updated, sources, tags, related) and add cross-references using [[wiki-links]]; 4) Update output/wiki/index.md with sections for Entities, Concepts, Sources, and Comparisons, listing and linking to the pages you created; 5) Append an operation log to output/log.md showing ingest, query, and lint actions with timestamps and brief summaries; 6) Run a lint-style health check and save a report to output/wiki/lint-report.md covering at least: Contradictions, Orphan Pages, Missing Pages, and Incomplete Metadata; 7) Answer the question "What are the top supply chain risks mentioned across these sources in 2025, and what mitigations do you recommend?" and save the analysis page to output/wiki/comparisons/supply-chain-risks-2025.md with YAML frontmatter (type=comparison, created, updated, compares: ["risks", "mitigations"]) and citations that link to [[sources/acme-2025-update]] and [[sources/supply-chain-brief]]. Use clear headings in pages (e.g., "Summary", "Key Points"), maintain consistent slugs, and do not modify any files under input/.
